articles.title: Letters, May 23, 2026: ‘Too many people in the national parks’
articles.description: Popularity problem There’s a problem in Canada’s national parks and that problem is there are too many people visiting the most popular places. articles.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
articles.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.
